title: Dot Product and Cross Product
summary: Calculate dot and cross products of vectors and apply them to find angles, projections, and normals.
content_html:
Two key vector multiplication operations: the dot product (scalar result) and the cross product (vector result).

Dot Product

uآ·v = uâ‚پvâ‚پ + uâ‚‚vâ‚‚ + uâ‚ƒvâ‚ƒ
Also: uآ·v = |u||v|cos(خ¸)

Example: u=(1,2,3), v=(4,5,6) â†’ uآ·v = 4+10+18 = 32

Key properties:

  • If uآ·v = 0, the vectors are perpendicular (orthogonal)
  • cos(خ¸) = (uآ·v)/(|u||v|) â€” finds angle between vectors

Projection

Projection of u onto v: projvu = (uآ·v/|v|آ²) أ— v

Cross Product (3D only)

u أ— v = (uâ‚‚vâ‚ƒâˆ’uâ‚ƒvâ‚‚, uâ‚ƒvâ‚پâˆ’uâ‚پvâ‚ƒ, uâ‚پvâ‚‚âˆ’uâ‚‚vâ‚پ)

Key properties:

  • Result is perpendicular to both u and v
  • |uأ—v| = |u||v|sin(خ¸) = area of parallelogram
  • NOT commutative: vأ—u = âˆ’(uأ—v)
  • If uأ—v = 0, vectors are parallel

exercises.title: Angle from dot product
exercises.prompt: The formula to find angle خ¸ between vectors u and v is:

exercises.title: Cross product result type
options: A vector perpendicular to both u and v
A unit vector in the same direction
exercises.answer: A vector perpendicular to both u and v

exercises.title: Cross product magnitude
options: Area of the parallelogram formed by u and v
Length of u + length of v
exercises.answer: Area of the parallelogram formed by u and v
